arts.ttl: Newcastle want Barcelona star as Lascelles replacement
arts.sum: Newcastle are looking at out-of-favour Barcelona defender, Eric Garcia, as a potential January replacement for Jamaal Lascelles. Eddie Howe has had injury problems with his centre-backs this season, and whilst Jamaal Lascelles has always been a key part of his plans, now could be the right time for Eric Garcia to step into the breach […] The post Newcastle want Barcelona star as Lascelles replacement appeared first on CaughtOffside.

arts.ttl: Report: Chelsea now want to sign one of Pep Guardiola's 'favourite' players from Barcelona
arts.sum: They can get him for just £20m

arts.ttl: ‘We want this’ - Pedri eager to give Barca fans early Xmas present
arts.sum: Barcelona midfielder Pedri says he’s eager to give fans an early Christmas present by beating Atletico Madrid on Saturday night. Barca will be without Lamine Yamal for the game due to injury but Pedri says the team must be able to cope without their teenaged superstar.

arts.ttl: Flick has just one selection dilemma for Barca vs Atleti
arts.sum: Diario Sport claim that Flick has decided to bring in either Ferran Torres or Fermin Lopez for the game but is yet to decide which player will get the nod. Would you go for Ferran Torres or Fermin Lopez against Atletico?

arts.ttl: 'How have Barca let him go?' Meet Chelsea's 18-year-old future 'star'
arts.sum: Marc Guiu, 18, could be a "big star" for Chelsea after scoring a hat-trick against Shamrock Rovers in the Conference League.

arts.ttl: Chelsea youngster Marc Guiu 'can go all the way', insists Joe Cole after his first-half hat-trick as former Blues star insists 'Barcelona have made a real mistake' letting him go
arts.sum: After emerging from the Catalan club's famed La Masia academy, Guiu arrived at Stamford Bridge in July after the Blues triggered the £5m release clause in his contract.

arts.ttl: Casado says Barca ‘know what to do’ to beat Atletico
arts.sum: Casado says Barcelona know what they need to do on Saturday to take all three points and wants his team to “at least” match Atletico’s intensity. Barcelona midfielder Marc Casado says his team know what they must do to improve ahead of Saturday’s match with Atletico Madrid.
